title: vitis bryoniifolia var. ternata
intro: Vitis bryoniifolia var. ternata, also known as the wild grape or Chinese wild grape, is a species of grapevine native to China and Southeast Asia. It is a deciduous climbing vine with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves, stems, and roots, which have been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory diseases, and also provide health benefits such as cardiovascular protection and immunomodulation. In herbal preparations, Vitis bryoniifolia var. ternata is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in combination with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicinal formulas.
